item.title: International Anti-Corruption Academy Partial Scholarship 2026: Apply for Tuition Support to Study the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS/IMACC)
item.dc:creator: Richard Nyirenda
item.description:
Published Date: 17 July 2026 The International Anti-Corruption Academy (IACA) is offering a limited number of Partial Scholarships for students applying to its 2026 Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS) or International Master in Anti-Corruption Compliance (IMACC) programmes. These scholarships are designed to support talented individuals from eligible developing countries who demonstrate both academic merit and […]

The post International Anti-Corruption Academy Partial Scholarship 2026: Apply for Tuition Support to Study the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS/IMACC) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Published Date: 17 July 2026

The International Anti-Corruption Academy (IACA) is offering a limited number of Partial Scholarships for students applying to its 2026 Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS) or International Master in Anti-Corruption Compliance (IMACC) programmes. These scholarships are designed to support talented individuals from eligible developing countries who demonstrate both academic merit and financial need, while also showing strong leadership potential and a commitment to creating positive change in their communities.

The scholarship provides partial financial assistance by covering tuition fees up to EUR 17,900, helping deserving students access high-quality education in anti-corruption, compliance, governance, and ethical leadership.

Individuals who aspire to become leaders in promoting transparency, integrity, accountability, and good governance are encouraged to apply.

About the IACA Partial Scholarship

The International Anti-Corruption Academy (IACA) recognizes that financial constraints can prevent outstanding candidates from pursuing postgraduate education. To address this challenge, the Academy provides a limited number of partial scholarships each year for qualified applicants enrolling in its master’s programmes.

These scholarships are awarded based on a combination of:

  • Academic merit
  • Financial need
  • Leadership potential
  • Capacity to successfully complete the programme
  • Commitment to promoting positive change in society

Rather than simply rewarding academic excellence, the scholarship aims to identify future leaders who can make meaningful contributions in combating corruption, strengthening compliance systems, and improving governance in both the public and private sectors.

Because scholarship funding is limited, applications undergo a competitive selection process.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive financial support toward their postgraduate studies.

The scholarship includes:

  • Tuition fee support of up to EUR 17,900
  • Opportunity to pursue the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS) or International Master in Anti-Corruption Compliance (IMACC)
  • Access to internationally recognized education in anti-corruption and compliance
  • Opportunity to study alongside professionals from around the world
  • Exposure to global best practices in governance, ethics, transparency, and compliance

The scholarship is intended to reduce the financial burden of tuition while enabling participants to strengthen their professional expertise and leadership capacity.

What the Scholarship Does Not Cover

Applicants should note that this is a partial scholarship, meaning it does not cover all study-related expenses.

Recipients must demonstrate that they can finance:

  • Remaining tuition fees not covered by the scholarship
  • Travel expenses (where applicable)
  • Accommodation
  • Living expenses
  • Visa-related costs
  • Insurance
  • Any additional expenses not covered by the scholarship

Applicants choosing the hybrid study format must also provide evidence that they can meet any travel and participation costs required by the programme.

Who Can Apply?

The scholarship is specifically designed for applicants from eligible developing countries.

Applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements established by IACA.

Eligible candidates must:

1. Nationality Requirement

Applicants must be citizens of:

  • Least Developed Countries (LDCs) as defined by the United Nations, or
  • IACA State Parties that are classified by the World Bank as:
    • Low-Income Economies
    • Lower-Middle-Income Economies
    • Upper-Middle-Income Economies

Only applicants from these eligible countries will be considered.

2. Admission Requirement

Applicants must have:

  • Submitted an application for admission to the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS) or International Master in Anti-Corruption Compliance (IMACC), or
  • Already been admitted into one of these programmes for the 2026 cohort.

Admission to the programme is an essential requirement before scholarship consideration.

3. Academic and Leadership Potential

Applicants must:

  • Meet all admission requirements for the master’s programme.
  • Demonstrate strong academic capability.
  • Show potential to complete the programme successfully within the two-year duration.
  • Demonstrate leadership qualities.
  • Show the ability to create positive social impact.
  • Display commitment to anti-corruption, compliance, transparency, governance, or related fields.

Scholarship Selection Criteria

Scholarships are awarded based on several factors, including:

Financial Need

Priority is given to applicants who genuinely require financial assistance to pursue their studies.

Academic Merit

Candidates should demonstrate the academic ability necessary to successfully complete the programme.

Leadership Potential

Applicants should possess strong leadership qualities and the ability to influence positive institutional and societal change.

Potential Impact

The selection committee seeks individuals who are likely to contribute significantly to anti-corruption efforts, ethical governance, and compliance initiatives after completing their studies.

Programme Completion Capacity

Candidates should demonstrate that they can complete the programme within its standard two-year duration.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should carefully complete the official scholarship application form.

Before submitting the scholarship application, applicants should ensure that they:

  • Complete every section accurately.
  • Follow all application instructions.
  • Provide truthful and complete information.
  • Upload all required supporting documents.

Incomplete applications may not be considered.

Important Application Requirement

Applicants cannot submit the scholarship application without an official IACA reference number.

This reference number is issued by the IACA Admissions Team after confirming that an applicant’s admission application is complete.

The reference number is sent via email and must be entered when completing the scholarship application form.

Applicants are therefore advised to complete their admission application early to allow sufficient time for verification and issuance of the reference number.

APPLY HERE

Why Apply for the IACA Partial Scholarship?

This scholarship offers an excellent opportunity for professionals and future leaders committed to integrity and good governance.

Benefits include:

  • Financial support toward postgraduate tuition
  • Internationally recognized master’s education
  • Specialized training in anti-corruption and compliance
  • Opportunities to strengthen leadership skills
  • Exposure to global governance best practices
  • Networking with international professionals
  • Enhanced career prospects in governance, compliance, public administration, law, development, and international organizations

Important Notes

Applicants should remember that:

  • Scholarship funding is limited.
  • Scholarships are awarded competitively.
  • Admission into the master’s programme is required before scholarship consideration.
  • Financial need alone does not guarantee selection.
  • Applicants must demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to creating positive societal impact.
  • Scholarship recipients remain responsible for expenses not covered by the award.

Application Deadline

No application deadline has been provided in the available information.

Applicants are encouraged to submit both their admission and scholarship applications as early as possible to avoid missing any institutional deadlines.

Visit HERE to learn more about International Anti-Corruption Academy Partial Scholarship

Conclusion

The IACA Partial Scholarship 2026 provides an outstanding opportunity for aspiring leaders from eligible developing countries to pursue advanced education in anti-corruption, compliance, and ethical governance. By offering tuition support of up to EUR 17,900, the scholarship helps reduce financial barriers while investing in individuals who have the potential to drive meaningful institutional and societal change.

Applicants who demonstrate academic excellence, financial need, leadership potential, and a strong commitment to integrity and transparency are strongly encouraged to apply. Since admission to the MACS or IMACC programme is a prerequisite for scholarship consideration, prospective students should begin the admissions process early and ensure they obtain their IACA reference number before submitting their scholarship application.

For more international scholarship opportunities, visit OFY HERE

The post International Anti-Corruption Academy Partial Scholarship 2026: Apply for Tuition Support to Study the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS/IMACC) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa Health Collaborative Research Fund 2026: Up to USD 22,000 in Funding for Early Career Researchers Advancing Primary Health Care in Sub-Saharan Africa
item.dc:creator: Bob Waudo
item.description:
The Africa Health Collaborative (AHC) Research Fund 2026 is now accepting Expressions of Interest (EOIs) from eligible Early Career Researchers (ECRs) seeking funding to develop innovative, collaborative research projects focused on strengthening Primary Health Care (PHC) systems across Sub-Saharan Africa. This prestigious funding opportunity aims to foster high-impact bilateral and multilateral research collaborations among member […]

The post Africa Health Collaborative Research Fund 2026: Up to USD 22,000 in Funding for Early Career Researchers Advancing Primary Health Care in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Africa Health Collaborative (AHC) Research Fund 2026 is now accepting Expressions of Interest (EOIs) from eligible Early Career Researchers (ECRs) seeking funding to develop innovative, collaborative research projects focused on strengthening Primary Health Care (PHC) systems across Sub-Saharan Africa.

This prestigious funding opportunity aims to foster high-impact bilateral and multilateral research collaborations among member institutions of the Africa Health Collaborative. Through this initiative, researchers will receive financial support to build research capacity, strengthen partnerships across African institutions, and generate evidence capable of influencing health policy, health financing, and human resource development.

Researchers with innovative ideas addressing health workforce challenges and financing policies in primary health care are encouraged to apply before the application deadline.

About the Africa Health Collaborative (AHC)

The Africa Health Collaborative (AHC) is a multi-year partnership dedicated to strengthening primary health care systems throughout Africa by investing in:

  • Health workforce development
  • Medical education
  • Innovation
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Research collaboration
  • Capacity building

The collaborative recognizes research as one of the most important pillars for improving healthcare delivery across the continent. Through this funding scheme, AHC seeks to promote translational research capable of producing practical solutions that positively impact African communities.

Rather than supporting isolated research projects, the program focuses on establishing sustainable partnerships that can continue attracting larger research grants in the future.

Overview of the 2026 Research Fund

The Africa Health Collaborative member institutions are jointly contributing matching funds to support collaborative research among their institutions.

The fund specifically supports:

  • Bilateral research projects involving two AHC member institutions.
  • Multilateral research projects involving three or more AHC institutions.

The overall objective is to empower Early Career Researchers by providing seed funding that enables them to:

  • Build international research collaborations.
  • Develop competitive research portfolios.
  • Generate preliminary findings.
  • Position themselves for larger external research grants.
  • Produce evidence that improves primary healthcare systems.

2026 Research Theme

The theme for the 2026 funding call is:

Health Human Resources Policy and Health Financing Policy in Primary Health Care in Sub-Saharan Africa

Applicants are expected to develop innovative research projects addressing policy-related issues affecting:

  • Health workforce development
  • Health worker distribution
  • Human resource planning
  • Primary healthcare financing
  • Sustainable healthcare systems
  • Universal Health Coverage
  • Community health systems
  • Policy implementation
  • Health systems strengthening

Projects should demonstrate clear potential for improving health outcomes within Sub-Saharan Africa.

Funding Value

Successful proposals can receive substantial financial support.

Bilateral Projects

Eligible projects may receive:

  • Up to USD 22,000
  • Each participating institution may award up to USD 11,000 to its own investigator.

Multilateral Projects

Funding will be scaled according to the number of participating institutions.

Each participating institution may receive:

  • Up to USD 11,000 for investigators affiliated with that institution.

Number of Projects to be Funded

The joint funding initiative plans to support:

  • Up to 9 collaborative research proposals

The funding period for each project will last:

  • 18 months

Project Duration

Approved research projects will:

  • Begin on January 1, 2027
  • Continue for a maximum period of 18 months

Eligible Research Activities

Funding may support numerous collaborative activities, including:

  • Joint research workshops
  • Researcher exchange visits
  • Collaborative research groups
  • Cross-institutional partnerships
  • Data collection
  • Field research
  • Research meetings
  • Capacity-building activities
  • Knowledge exchange
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Publication activities (where institutionally permitted)

The program emphasizes collaboration among institutions and encourages interdisciplinary approaches.

What the Funding Can Cover

Depending on institutional guidelines, eligible expenses may include:

  • International and domestic travel
  • Flights
  • Hotel accommodation
  • Subsistence costs
  • Workshops
  • Research meetings
  • Research consumables (up to USD 1,500 where applicable)
  • Graduate student stipends
  • Research exchanges
  • Field visits
  • Software licenses
  • External consultation fees
  • Publishing costs
  • Conference presentations (institution-dependent)
  • Data collection
  • Research dissemination activities

Costs That Are Generally Not Eligible

The program generally excludes funding for:

  • Equipment purchases
  • Replacement teaching costs
  • Replacement research costs
  • Directly allocated applicant salaries
  • Estate and indirect costs
  • Research assistants (institution-dependent)
  • Entertainment expenses
  • Excessive branding
  • Non-research marketing activities

Eligible Institutions

Only Early Career Researchers affiliated with the following Africa Health Collaborative institutions may apply:

  • Addis Ababa University
  • African Institute for Mathematical Sciences (AIMS)
  • African Leadership University
  • Amref International University
  • Ashesi University
  • Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology
  • Moi University
  • University of Cape Town
  • University of Toronto

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must satisfy several important requirements.

Research Leadership

Applications must be led by an Early Career Researcher (ECR).

Collaboration

Projects must be either:

  • Bilateral (two participating institutions), or
  • Multilateral (three or more participating institutions)

Student Participation

Every proposal must include participation by:

  • Master’s students
  • Doctoral students
  • Postdoctoral trainees

Application Limits

Applicants may lead only one application.

Applicants Without Co-applicants

Researchers who do not yet have collaborators may still submit an application.

If approved by their home institution, their proposal may be placed in a public repository to help identify suitable collaborators from other AHC member institutions.

External Collaborators

Researchers from:

  • Government
  • NGOs
  • International universities
  • Community organizations
  • Other research institutions

may participate in projects but cannot serve as principal applicants.

Institutional Definitions of Early Career Researchers

Each institution has its own definition of an Early Career Researcher.

Examples include:

  • Faculty within a specified number of years after PhD completion.
  • Assistant professors.
  • Lecturers.
  • Postdoctoral researchers.
  • Newly appointed researchers.
  • PhD graduates within defined timeframes.
  • PhD students meeting publication requirements.
  • Master’s graduates with peer-reviewed publications (institution dependent).

Applicants should carefully review the requirements applicable to their institution before submitting an application.

Equality, Equity, Diversity and Inclusion

The Africa Health Collaborative strongly promotes Equality, Equity, Diversity and Inclusion (EDI).

Applicant teams are encouraged to foster research environments that:

  • Respect diversity
  • Promote inclusion
  • Encourage different perspectives
  • Support persons with disabilities
  • Promote gender equity
  • Recognize indigenous peoples
  • Include diverse sectors and disciplines

Evaluation Criteria

Applications will be assessed by a joint review committee representing all participating institutions.

Evaluation will consider:

  1. Project design
  2. Research rationale
  3. Academic quality
  4. Complementarity among collaborators
  5. Potential translational impact
  6. Demonstrable societal impact
  7. Potential for future funding
  8. Alignment with Africa Health Collaborative priorities

Application Process

The application process consists of two stages.

Stage One: Expression of Interest (EOI)

Applicants first submit an Expression of Interest demonstrating:

  • Eligibility
  • Research concept
  • Institutional affiliation

Successful EOIs will receive an invitation to submit a full proposal.

Stage Two: Full Proposal

Only approved applicants from Stage One will be invited to submit comprehensive research proposals.

One joint proposal will be submitted on behalf of the project team.

Important Dates

  • Applications Open: Currently Open
  • Expression of Interest Deadline: 15 August 2026 (12:00 PM GMT)
  • EOI Results Announcement: 7 September 2026 (12:00 PM GMT)
  • Invitation for Full Proposals: 7 November 2026 (12:00 PM GMT)
  • Project Start Date: 1 January 2027

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Confirm eligibility through their institution.
  2. Prepare an Expression of Interest.
  3. Submit the EOI before the deadline.
  4. Await institutional review.
  5. If invited, prepare and submit the full proposal.

Applicants requiring clarification may contact: africasecretariat@utoronto.ca

APPLY HERE

Why You Should Apply

The Africa Health Collaborative Research Fund represents an outstanding opportunity for emerging researchers to establish long-term international collaborations while addressing some of the most pressing healthcare challenges facing Sub-Saharan Africa.

Beyond financial support, successful applicants gain access to an expanding network of leading African and international universities committed to advancing primary healthcare research. The collaborative nature of the program also enhances researchers’ competitiveness for future international funding, strengthens institutional partnerships, and contributes to evidence-based policymaking that can improve health systems across the continent.

For Early Career Researchers seeking to build impactful research careers while contributing to stronger primary healthcare systems, this fund offers an excellent platform for professional growth and meaningful scientific collaboration.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Africa Health Collaborative Research Fund 2026

Conclusion

The Africa Health Collaborative Research Fund 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for Early Career Researchers to contribute meaningfully to the future of primary health care in Sub-Saharan Africa. By providing substantial funding, encouraging cross-institutional collaboration, and supporting innovative research on health human resources and health financing policies, the program aims to generate practical solutions that can strengthen health systems and improve healthcare delivery across the region.

Beyond the financial support of up to USD 22,000 for bilateral projects, the initiative offers researchers a valuable platform to establish international partnerships, build research capacity, mentor graduate trainees, and develop competitive projects capable of attracting larger grants in the future. The emphasis on translational research ensures that funded projects will not only advance academic knowledge but also contribute to evidence-based policies and sustainable improvements in primary health care.

Eligible Early Career Researchers affiliated with participating Africa Health Collaborative member institutions are encouraged to begin preparing their Expressions of Interest well in advance of the 15 August 2026 (12:00 PM GMT) deadline. Applicants should carefully review their institution-specific eligibility requirements, develop strong collaborative partnerships, and design impactful research proposals that align with the 2026 funding theme. For researchers committed to advancing health systems and creating lasting impact across Africa, this grant represents an exceptional opportunity to transform innovative ideas into meaningful change.

For more global Grant opportunities visit OFY HERE

The post Africa Health Collaborative Research Fund 2026: Up to USD 22,000 in Funding for Early Career Researchers Advancing Primary Health Care in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD Programme 2026 at the Lithuanian Centre for Social Sciences: Apply to Research Migration, Housing, Segregation and Regional Inequalities
item.dc:creator: Richard Nyirenda
item.description:
Published Date: 17 July 2026 The Lithuanian Centre for Social Sciences (LCSS) is inviting applications from motivated and aspiring researchers to join its fully funded PhD programme. This opportunity is ideal for candidates who are passionate about understanding the social, economic, and spatial dynamics shaping societies and are eager to contribute to evidence-based research with […]

The post Fully Funded PhD Programme 2026 at the Lithuanian Centre for Social Sciences: Apply to Research Migration, Housing, Segregation and Regional Inequalities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Published Date: 17 July 2026

The Lithuanian Centre for Social Sciences (LCSS) is inviting applications from motivated and aspiring researchers to join its fully funded PhD programme. This opportunity is ideal for candidates who are passionate about understanding the social, economic, and spatial dynamics shaping societies and are eager to contribute to evidence-based research with real-world impact.

The successful PhD student will work closely with a researcher whose work focuses on socio-economic segregation, internal migration, suburbanization, housing, and regional inequalities. The programme offers an excellent environment for candidates interested in quantitative social science research, advanced statistical analysis, and spatial data methods.

Applications are open until 24 August 2026.

About the PhD Programme

The PhD position is hosted by the Lithuanian Centre for Social Sciences, one of Lithuania’s leading research institutions dedicated to advancing knowledge across a wide range of social science disciplines.

The selected doctoral researcher will undertake an original research project that aligns closely with the supervisor’s areas of expertise while also developing an independent research profile. The programme emphasizes rigorous empirical research supported by extensive datasets and modern analytical techniques.

Unlike many doctoral programmes that focus primarily on theoretical discussions, this PhD provides an opportunity to work with rich national datasets to answer important questions affecting society, regional development, housing markets, and population dynamics.

The programme is available in both English and Lithuanian, allowing international academic participation while maintaining strong engagement with local research contexts.

Research Areas

Applicants are expected to develop research projects related to one or more of the following themes:

  • Socio-economic segregation
  • Internal migration
  • Population mobility
  • Suburbanization
  • Housing markets
  • Housing inequalities
  • Regional development
  • Regional inequalities
  • Spatial social science
  • Urban and regional studies
  • Demographic change
  • Quantitative social research

These topics address some of the most pressing societal challenges in Lithuania and across Europe, making the research highly relevant for policymakers, urban planners, academics, and public institutions.

Focus on Data-Driven Research

A distinctive feature of this PhD programme is its strong emphasis on quantitative research methods.

Doctoral researchers will work extensively with data to investigate social and regional processes using advanced analytical techniques.

Research methods may include:

  • Statistical analysis
  • Quantitative research methods
  • Spatial analysis
  • Geographic Information Systems (GIS)
  • Data visualization
  • Mapping and spatial modelling
  • Population and demographic analysis

Lithuania possesses an extensive and high-quality national data infrastructure that remains underutilized in many areas of social science research. This creates significant opportunities for doctoral researchers to produce innovative findings with practical relevance.

The programme encourages students to uncover new insights that can contribute to academic knowledge while informing public policy and regional planning.

Research Environment

The successful applicant will become part of a supportive and collaborative academic environment that values innovation, independence, and interdisciplinary research.

According to the supervising researcher, the programme offers:

  • A supportive supervision environment
  • Opportunities to conduct meaningful research
  • Academic freedom
  • Access to valuable research datasets
  • Opportunities for international collaboration
  • An intellectually stimulating research community

Doctoral candidates will have the opportunity to engage with scholars working on related topics while developing skills that are valuable in academia, government, research organizations, and international institutions.

Why This PhD Programme Is Worth Considering

This opportunity offers several advantages for aspiring researchers:

Fully Funded Doctoral Study

The selected candidate will join a fully funded PhD programme, allowing them to focus on research without the financial burden commonly associated with doctoral education.

Real-World Research Impact

The research addresses important social issues that influence housing policy, migration trends, regional development, and socio-economic inequalities.

Access to High-Quality Data

Lithuania offers an exceptional national data infrastructure, providing researchers with valuable datasets capable of generating meaningful academic and policy insights.

International Collaboration

The programme supports international academic engagement, creating opportunities to collaborate with researchers from other countries and institutions.

Academic Independence

Students are encouraged to pursue original research questions while receiving guidance from an experienced supervisor.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is particularly suitable for motivated individuals who have an interest in:

  • Social sciences
  • Human geography
  • Urban studies
  • Regional studies
  • Demography
  • Economics
  • Sociology
  • Housing studies
  • Migration studies
  • Spatial analysis
  • Public policy
  • Quantitative research

Applicants should demonstrate enthusiasm for research and an interest in working with large datasets and statistical methods.

Language Requirements

Although the programme is available in both English and Lithuanian, the research will involve extensive engagement with Lithuanian datasets and local contexts.

For this reason, the following would be considered a strong advantage:

  • Lithuanian language skills
  • Familiarity with Lithuania
  • Understanding of Lithuanian society and regional context

These qualities are not explicitly stated as mandatory but are expected to strengthen an applicant’s suitability for the position.

APPLY HERE

Programme Highlights

Successful applicants will benefit from:

  • Fully funded doctoral studies
  • Research on important societal challenges
  • Strong supervision
  • Quantitative and spatial research training
  • Statistical analysis experience
  • GIS and mapping opportunities
  • Access to extensive national datasets
  • Academic freedom
  • International collaboration opportunities
  • Supportive research environment

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

24 August 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the closing date to avoid missing this opportunity.

How to Apply

Candidates should submit their application through the official application portal before the deadline.

Applicants should carefully review the programme requirements and prepare all required documents before submitting their application.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Fully Funded PhD Programme 2026 at the Lithuanian Centre for Social Sciences

Conclusion

The Fully Funded PhD Programme at the Lithuanian Centre for Social Sciences offers an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers who wish to build expertise in socio-economic segregation, migration, housing, suburbanization, and regional inequalities. Through its emphasis on quantitative research, statistical analysis, spatial methods, and the use of Lithuania’s rich national datasets, the programme provides an excellent foundation for producing impactful research with both academic and policy relevance.

Beyond funding, participants will benefit from supportive supervision, academic independence, access to high-quality data, and opportunities for international collaboration. Individuals with strong research interests in social sciences and quantitative methods, particularly those familiar with Lithuania and its language, are encouraged to apply before the application deadline of 24 August 2026.

For more international scholarship opportunities, visit OFY HERE

The post Fully Funded PhD Programme 2026 at the Lithuanian Centre for Social Sciences: Apply to Research Migration, Housing, Segregation and Regional Inequalities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2027 Sloan Research Fellowships in USA and Canada | $75,000 for Early-Career Researchers
item.dc:creator: Evelyn Essuman
item.description:
The 2027 Sloan Research Fellowships are now open for nominations. Offered by the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation, the prestigious fellowship recognizes outstanding early-career faculty members whose research demonstrates exceptional creativity, innovation, and the potential to become future leaders in science. Successful fellows receive $75,000 to support their research over a two-year period. Each year, the […]

The post 2027 Sloan Research Fellowships in USA and Canada | $75,000 for Early-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2027 Sloan Research Fellowships are now open for nominations. Offered by the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation, the prestigious fellowship recognizes outstanding early-career faculty members whose research demonstrates exceptional creativity, innovation, and the potential to become future leaders in science. Successful fellows receive $75,000 to support their research over a two-year period.

Each year, the Sloan Research Fellowships identify promising researchers who are making significant contributions to their disciplines while still in the early stages of their academic careers. Beyond the financial award, the fellowship is internationally recognized as one of the most prestigious honors for young scientists and scholars, helping recipients strengthen their research programs and accelerate their careers.

The 2027 Sloan Research Fellowships are open to eligible faculty members in the United States and Canada working in chemistry, computer science, Earth system science, economics, mathematics, neuroscience, and physics.

About the Sloan Research Fellowships

Established by the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation, the Sloan Research Fellowships have supported thousands of exceptional researchers who later became leaders in academia, industry, and public service. Many former Sloan Fellows have gone on to receive major international honors, including Nobel Prizes and other prestigious scientific awards.

The fellowship is designed to recognize researchers with outstanding independent accomplishments and provide flexible funding that enables them to pursue innovative research ideas during a critical stage of their careers.

Rather than funding a specific research project, the Sloan Research Fellowship provides unrestricted research support to the recipient’s institution, allowing fellows to use the award where it will have the greatest impact on their research.

Fellowship Value

Each selected fellow receives:

  • $75,000 fellowship
  • Funding awarded to the fellow’s institution
  • Two-year funding period
  • Flexible use of funds to support research activities

The fellowship funding may be used for:

  • Research equipment
  • Technical assistance
  • Summer salary support
  • Professional travel
  • Graduate or trainee support
  • Other research-related activities

Eligible Fields

Applications are accepted from researchers working in:

  • Chemistry
  • Computer Science
  • Earth System Science
  • Economics
  • Mathematics
  • Neuroscience
  • Physics

Researchers in closely related disciplines may also be considered where appropriate.

Eligibility Criteria

To be eligible for the 2027 Sloan Research Fellowships, candidates must:

  • Hold a Ph.D. or an equivalent doctoral degree.
  • Be members of the faculty at a college, university, or other degree-granting institution in the United States or Canada.
  • Hold a tenure-track, but untenured, faculty appointment as of September 15, 2026.
  • Have a regular teaching obligation as part of their faculty appointment.
  • Demonstrate outstanding independent research accomplishments, creativity, initiative, and strong leadership potential.

The Alfred P. Sloan Foundation strongly encourages nominations of qualified candidates from all backgrounds.

Required Nomination Materials

Applicants should prepare the following documents:

  • One confidential nomination letter
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Two journal articles
  • Research statement
  • Three confidential letters of support

Applicants will also be required to complete the following online forms:

  • Nominee General Information
  • Nominator Information
  • Support Writer Information
  • EEO-1/Affirmative Action Voluntary Self-Identification Form

Selection Criteria

Candidates are evaluated based on their:

  • Research excellence
  • Independent scientific accomplishments
  • Creativity and innovation
  • Leadership potential
  • Long-term impact on their field

The fellowship recognizes researchers who have already demonstrated exceptional promise and are expected to become leaders within the international scientific community.

Important Dates

  • Applications Open: July 15, 2026
  • Application Deadline: September 15, 2026 (11:59 PM EDT)

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their nomination materials well before the deadline to allow sufficient time for referees and institutional approvals.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted through the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation’s online application system.

Applicants should carefully review all eligibility requirements and prepare the required nomination documents before beginning the online application.

Official Website: https://sloan.org/fellowships/sloan-research-fellowships

Application Portal: https://apsloan.smapply.io

Why You Should Apply

Receiving a Sloan Research Fellowship is widely regarded as one of the most prestigious recognitions available to early-career faculty members in science and related disciplines. In addition to the $75,000 award, fellows join a distinguished network of researchers whose work has shaped scientific discovery around the world.

The fellowship provides the flexibility to invest in research priorities, strengthen laboratory capacity, support trainees, purchase equipment, or pursue new scientific directions. For promising researchers seeking to expand the impact of their work, the Sloan Research Fellowship represents an exceptional opportunity to advance both their research and academic careers.

Conclusion

The 2027 Sloan Research Fellowships provide an outstanding opportunity for exceptional early-career faculty members in the United States and Canada to receive $75,000 in research funding and gain recognition as emerging leaders in their fields. If you meet the eligibility requirements and have demonstrated excellence in research, this fellowship can provide valuable support to advance your scientific career. Interested candidates should begin preparing their nomination materials early and submit their applications before September 15, 2026 

Discover more fellowship opportunities like these on : https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/category/fellowship/

 

The post 2027 Sloan Research Fellowships in USA and Canada | $75,000 for Early-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of California, Irvine Postdoctoral Scholar in Clinical Neuroscience Research 2026–2027: Earn Up to $82,836
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Applications are now open for the University of California, Irvine Postdoctoral Scholar – Clinical Neuroscience Research position, offering qualified early-career researchers an opportunity to advance their expertise at the intersection of clinical neurology, neuroscience, artificial intelligence, machine learning, data science, and digital health. Based in the Department of Neurology within the University of California, Irvine […]

The post University of California, Irvine Postdoctoral Scholar in Clinical Neuroscience Research 2026–2027: Earn Up to $82,836 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the University of California, Irvine Postdoctoral Scholar – Clinical Neuroscience Research position, offering qualified early-career researchers an opportunity to advance their expertise at the intersection of clinical neurology, neuroscience, artificial intelligence, machine learning, data science, and digital health.

Based in the Department of Neurology within the University of California, Irvine School of Medicine, this in-person postdoctoral opportunity is designed for researchers interested in addressing important questions in clinical and translational neuroscience using advanced computational and statistical approaches.

The successful candidate will join a multidisciplinary clinical neurology and neuroinformatics research group under the direct supervision of Dr. Ali Ezzati, M.D. They will work alongside faculty and researchers with expertise spanning neurology, epidemiology, data science, and biostatistics.

Applications opened on May 11, 2026. Candidates seeking full consideration should apply by the next review date of July 31, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. Pacific Time. The final application deadline is May 10, 2027, at 11:59 p.m. Pacific Time. Applications submitted after the review date will only be considered if the position remains unfilled.

About the University of California, Irvine Postdoctoral Scholar Position

The Postdoctoral Scholar – Clinical Neuroscience Research position provides an opportunity to participate in advanced research involving Alzheimer’s disease and other dementias while contributing to broader areas of clinical neurology and neuroinformatics.

The research group specializes in Alzheimer’s disease and other forms of dementia, with additional research interests covering:

  • Migraine and other headache disorders
  • Neuroepidemiology
  • Neurodegenerative disorders
  • Artificial intelligence
  • Machine learning
  • Advanced biostatistical modelling
  • Neuroinformatics
  • Digital health
  • Translational neuroscience

Researchers in the laboratory use advanced analytical and computational methods to investigate pressing questions in clinical neurology.

The selected Postdoctoral Scholar will have opportunities to work with large biomedical datasets derived from cohort studies and clinical trials. The position combines clinical research with modern approaches in data science, making it particularly relevant to researchers seeking careers in clinical and translational neuroscience, digital health, neuroinformatics, or biomedical data science.

Salary and Financial Benefits

The salary range for the University of California, Irvine Postdoctoral Scholar position is:

$69,073 to $82,836

The applicable salary is determined according to the University of California Postdoctoral Scholar salary scale and the candidate’s experience level.

In certain circumstances, off-scale salaries that exceed the published system-wide salary at a designated experience level may be offered when necessary to meet competitive conditions.

This competitive salary structure provides successful candidates with an opportunity to build advanced research experience while working within a major academic and medical research environment.

Key Responsibilities of the Postdoctoral Scholar

The successful applicant will undertake a combination of data analysis, scientific research, academic publishing, research development, collaboration, and mentorship responsibilities.

Key responsibilities include:

1. Analyzing Large-Scale Biomedical Datasets

The Postdoctoral Scholar will analyze complex datasets generated through research cohorts and clinical trials.

This work may involve longitudinal data and other large-scale biomedical datasets requiring advanced statistical and computational approaches.

2. Developing Predictive Models and Analytical Tools

The successful candidate will develop and implement predictive models and advanced analytical tools to address research questions in clinical neuroscience.

Depending on the research project, this work may incorporate methods from:

  • Artificial intelligence
  • Machine learning
  • Biostatistics
  • Epidemiology
  • Data science
  • Neuroinformatics

The position therefore offers substantial opportunities for researchers interested in applying computational methods to real-world clinical and biomedical challenges.

3. Publishing Research Findings

The Postdoctoral Scholar will summarize research findings and contribute to the preparation and publication of scientific papers in peer-reviewed journals.

Applicants are expected to have already demonstrated strong scientific communication skills through previous peer-reviewed publications and conference presentations.

The opportunity to continue publishing research can help successful candidates strengthen their academic profiles and establish expertise within clinical neuroscience and related disciplines.

4. Contributing to Grant Writing

The selected researcher will contribute to grant-writing activities and support the development of new clinical research studies.

This responsibility provides valuable exposure to research funding processes and the development of competitive scientific proposals.

Experience in grant writing can be particularly valuable for researchers planning long-term careers in academia, biomedical research, clinical research, or research institutions.

5. Mentoring Junior Researchers

The position includes opportunities to provide mentorship to junior researchers.

The successful candidate may support less experienced researchers and contribute to their professional and scientific development.

6. Supporting Laboratory Operations

In addition to conducting research, the Postdoctoral Scholar will help oversee laboratory operations.

This responsibility provides experience in research coordination and laboratory management alongside the candidate’s scientific work.

7. Collaborating on Interdisciplinary Research Projects

The successful candidate will collaborate with faculty members and researchers on projects involving areas such as:

  • Biomarkers
  • Digital health platforms
  • Neuroinformatics
  • Clinical neurology
  • Epidemiology
  • Data science
  • Biostatistics

The multidisciplinary nature of the research environment allows the Postdoctoral Scholar to work across traditional academic and scientific boundaries.

Research Areas Covered by the Position

The University of California, Irvine clinical neurology and neuroinformatics research group focuses primarily on Alzheimer’s disease and other dementias.

However, its research interests extend to several interconnected fields, including migraine and headache disorders, neuroepidemiology, and other neurodegenerative conditions.

The group applies advanced methods such as artificial intelligence, machine learning, neuroinformatics, and biostatistical modelling to investigate clinical neurological questions.

This combination of medicine, neuroscience, data science, and technology makes the opportunity particularly relevant to researchers interested in emerging interdisciplinary areas of biomedical research.

Basic Eligibility and Qualification Requirements

Applicants must meet several essential requirements at the time of application.

Candidates should have:

  • A recent or anticipated PhD or equivalent degree obtained or expected within the last three years.
  • Expertise in neuroscience, epidemiology, computer science, statistics, mathematics, or a related discipline.
  • Proficiency in R and/or Python programming.
  • Strong written and oral communication skills.
  • A record of peer-reviewed publications and conference presentations demonstrating scientific communication capabilities.

Because the opportunity combines biomedical research with computational analysis, applicants should be comfortable working with complex datasets and applying analytical approaches to scientific research questions.

Preferred Qualifications

Although not mandatory, applicants with additional experience in relevant research and technical areas may be particularly competitive.

Preferred qualifications include:

  • A background in programming.
  • Experience or knowledge in biostatistics.
  • Experience in clinical research.
  • Knowledge of cognitive neurology.
  • Knowledge of neurodegenerative diseases.
  • Expertise or knowledge in epidemiology.
  • Knowledge of headache medicine.

Candidates whose backgrounds combine quantitative expertise with clinical or neuroscience research experience may be particularly well positioned for the multidisciplinary nature of the role.

Application Requirements

Applicants must prepare and submit the required application materials through the University of California, Irvine Academic Personnel Recruit platform.

The required documents include:

  1. Cover Letter – Applicants should prepare a tailored cover letter explaining their relevant academic background, research experience, technical expertise, and interest in the position.
  2. Curriculum Vitae – Candidates must submit their most recently updated curriculum vitae highlighting their education, research experience, publications, conference presentations, technical skills, and other relevant achievements.
  3. Three References – Applicants must provide contact information for three references.

Candidates should ensure that all submitted information is accurate and that their application clearly demonstrates how their research and technical experience aligns with the laboratory’s focus areas.

Application Deadline and Important Dates

The application timeline is as follows:

  • Application opening date: May 11, 2026
  • Next review date for full consideration: July 31, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. Pacific Time
  • Final application deadline: May 10, 2027, at 11:59 p.m. Pacific Time

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications by July 31, 2026, to ensure full consideration by the selection committee.

Although applications will continue to be accepted until the final deadline of May 10, 2027, applications received after the review date will only be considered if the position has not already been filled.

Applying early is therefore highly advisable.

Job Location

The Postdoctoral Scholar – Clinical Neuroscience Research position is an in-person role based at the University of California, Irvine campus in Irvine, California, United States.

Applicants should take the location requirement into consideration before submitting their applications.

How to Apply

Interested and qualified candidates must submit their applications through the official University of California, Irvine Academic Personnel Recruit portal.

APPLY HERE

Candidates should carefully review the application requirements and prepare an updated curriculum vitae, a tailored cover letter, and contact information for three references before submitting their application.

Equal Employment Opportunity

The University of California, Irvine is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, age, protected veteran status, or other categories protected under the University of California’s anti-discrimination policies.

The finalist selected for the position will also be required to disclose whether they have been subject to any final administrative or judicial decisions within the previous seven years determining that they committed misconduct.

Successful employees are required to comply with applicable University policies, collective bargaining agreements, and any relevant federal, state, or local government requirements.

Final Thoughts

The University of California, Irvine Postdoctoral Scholar – Clinical Neuroscience Research position offers a valuable opportunity for early-career researchers seeking advanced experience in clinical neuroscience, Alzheimer’s disease and dementia research, neuroinformatics, artificial intelligence, machine learning, biostatistics, and digital health.

With an annual salary range of $69,073 to $82,836, access to large biomedical datasets, opportunities to contribute to peer-reviewed publications and grant development, and collaboration with experts from multiple disciplines, the position can provide a strong foundation for researchers pursuing careers in clinical and translational neuroscience, biomedical data science, academic research, and digital health.

Qualified candidates should prioritize submitting their applications by the July 31, 2026 review date to receive full consideration rather than waiting for the final application deadline of May 10, 2027.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more Global Job Opportunities here.

The post University of California, Irvine Postdoctoral Scholar in Clinical Neuroscience Research 2026–2027: Earn Up to $82,836 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: European Fund for the Balkans Common Ground Program Opportunity and Innovation Grants 2026: Apply for Up to €4,500
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The European Fund for the Balkans is accepting applications for the 2026 Common Ground Program Opportunity and Innovation Grants, a funding opportunity designed to support civil society organizations and community initiatives working to bridge divides, strengthen dialogue, build trust, and promote social cohesion across the Western Balkans. Through the Opportunity and Innovation Grants, selected organizations […]

The post European Fund for the Balkans Common Ground Program Opportunity and Innovation Grants 2026: Apply for Up to €4,500 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Fund for the Balkans is accepting applications for the 2026 Common Ground Program Opportunity and Innovation Grants, a funding opportunity designed to support civil society organizations and community initiatives working to bridge divides, strengthen dialogue, build trust, and promote social cohesion across the Western Balkans.

Through the Opportunity and Innovation Grants, selected organizations can receive up to €4,500 to test promising new ideas, respond to emerging opportunities and challenges, and develop innovative approaches to fostering meaningful dialogue between local communities.

A total of 20 grants will be available in 2026. Applications are considered on a rolling basis, meaning eligible organizations are strongly encouraged to submit their proposals as early as possible before all available grants have been awarded.

The application deadline is November 2, 2026, at 23:59 CET, subject to the availability of grants.

About the Opportunity and Innovation Grants

The Opportunity and Innovation Grants are offered under the Common Ground Program and are designed to help civil society organizations leverage relatively modest amounts of funding to achieve potentially larger and longer-term impacts.

The grants particularly focus on incubating promising new approaches, including new topics, methodologies, interventions, and formats that can strengthen dialogue between communities in the Western Balkans.

Rather than primarily financing organizations’ routine activities, the program seeks to support targeted opportunities and innovative ideas that respond to specific community needs or emerging developments.

Potential activities supported through the grants may include:

  • Preparing an application for a larger funding opportunity.
  • Conducting a pilot project to test a new approach or methodology.
  • Undertaking a feasibility study for a potentially larger initiative.
  • Responding quickly to political developments affecting local communities.
  • Taking proactive action in anticipation of political or social developments.
  • Testing new approaches to community dialogue and reconciliation.
  • Developing initiatives that can bridge divisions and strengthen trust between different groups.

Applicants will be required to clearly identify the local community they intend to work with and explain the particular divide their proposed initiative seeks to address.

These divisions may relate to identities including national, ethnic, racial, religious, gender, or sexual orientation, among others.

Grant Amount and Number of Available Grants

Successful applicants may receive a maximum grant of €4,500.

A total of 20 Opportunity and Innovation Grants will be available during 2026.

Because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and the number of available grants is limited, organizations with suitable ideas should consider applying as early as possible rather than waiting until the final deadline.

What Can the Grant Support?

The program provides flexibility for applicants to develop interventions that respond to their specific local contexts. The central requirement is that the proposed activity should represent a genuine opportunity or an innovative approach to bridging divides, building trust, or strengthening social cohesion.

Examples of possible interventions include pilot initiatives, community dialogue activities, feasibility studies, responses to emerging political or social developments, and preparatory work that could unlock larger future funding opportunities.

Applicants will need to demonstrate how their proposed intervention goes beyond ordinary organizational activities and explain why the idea represents a meaningful opportunity or innovation within the community concerned.

Application and Implementation Timeline

Applications for the Opportunity and Innovation Grants are accepted on a rolling basis, subject to the availability of the 20 grants allocated for 2026.

The stated application deadline is November 2, 2026, at 23:59 CET. However, because grants may be awarded before the final deadline, interested organizations are advised to apply early.

According to the call information, funded activities must be implemented by November 1, 2026.

Successful grant recipients or their fiscal hosts will subsequently be required to submit their final narrative and financial reports by December 1, 2026.

Applicants should carefully consider these timelines when designing their proposed activities and ensure that their projects can realistically be completed within the required implementation period.

How Applications Will Be Evaluated

Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis by at least two European Fund for the Balkans staff members. Proposals will be assessed according to four main criteria.

1. Strategic and Values Fit – 30%

This criterion accounts for 30% of the overall assessment.

Strong applicants are expected to be small and medium-sized local organizations, collectives, or ad hoc groups working to bridge divides within a specific local context.

Projects may also address issues or themes with the potential to unite communities, strengthen relationships, and build trust among different groups.

Applicants should therefore clearly demonstrate how their proposed initiative aligns with the broader goals of dialogue, peace, social cohesion, and community trust-building.

2. Opportunity or Innovation Argumentation – 30%

Another 30% of the assessment focuses on the strength of the applicant’s opportunity or innovation argument.

Applicants will be expected to provide a concise but convincing explanation of:

  • The context or challenge they want to address.
  • The proposed intervention.
  • The activities they intend to undertake.
  • Why the proposed approach represents an innovative or promising response to the identified problem.

A strong application should clearly connect the problem, proposed intervention, and expected results. Applicants should avoid simply describing routine organizational activities and instead demonstrate what makes their proposed approach timely, innovative, or particularly promising.

3. Estimated Impact – 25%

The estimated impact of the proposed intervention accounts for 25% of the evaluation.

Evaluators will consider how likely the proposed project is to produce the intended social results.

Applicants should therefore clearly explain the changes they expect their intervention to generate and demonstrate a realistic connection between their planned activities and the expected impact on the targeted community.

4. Feasibility and Proportionality – 15%

The remaining 15% of the evaluation focuses on feasibility and proportionality.

While all types of costs are considered admissible, applicants must explain how individual budget items directly contribute to the specific opportunity or innovation being proposed.

The grant should not simply subsidize the applicant organization’s everyday operations. Instead, the budget should be clearly connected to the proposed intervention and proportionate to the activities and anticipated results.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit an application form alongside several supporting documents.

The required documentation includes:

  1. Valid registration documentation for the applicant organization or fiscal host.
  2. The statute of the applicant organization or fiscal host.
  3. A list of staff members and their respective roles within the applicant organization or fiscal host.
  4. A completed budget using the required budget template.
  5. For coalitions or organizations working through fiscal hosts, a duly signed Memorandum of Understanding between the relevant parties.

The Memorandum of Understanding should clearly establish the distribution of roles and responsibilities between the participating parties, including arrangements governing the management and use of grant funds.

Applicants should ensure that all required documents are complete and properly prepared before submitting their applications.

Application Decision Timeline

The European Fund for the Balkans aims, in principle, to communicate application decisions within five working days after an application has been submitted.

This relatively short decision timeline may make the grant particularly valuable for organizations seeking to respond quickly to emerging opportunities, political developments, or urgent community needs.

If the review process is delayed, applicants will be informed accordingly.

Reporting Requirements for Successful Applicants

Organizations that receive funding, or their fiscal hosts where applicable, will be required to submit both narrative and financial reports.

The narrative report should describe the activities implemented and place particular emphasis on the specific opportunity or innovation supported through the grant.

A basic financial report detailing the use of the awarded funds will also be required.

The deadline for submitting final narrative and financial reports is December 1, 2026.

Grant recipients should therefore maintain appropriate records throughout project implementation to facilitate accurate and timely reporting.

Key Dates to Remember

  • Applications: Rolling basis while grants remain available.
  • Final application deadline: November 2, 2026, at 23:59 CET.
  • Activity implementation deadline: November 1, 2026.
  • Final narrative and financial reporting deadline: December 1, 2026.
  • Number of grants available in 2026: 20.
  • Maximum grant amount: €4,500.

How to Apply

Eligible organizations should complete the official application form and prepare all required supporting documentation, including the mandatory budget template.

Applications should be submitted through the official Opportunity and Innovation Grants application portal before the deadline and, preferably, as early as possible because applications are assessed on a rolling basis and only 20 grants are available.

APPLY HERE

Organizations requiring clarification regarding the call can contact the Common Ground Program team at cgp@balkanfund.org.

With funding of up to €4,500 and a flexible focus on innovative and timely community interventions, the European Fund for the Balkans Common Ground Program Opportunity and Innovation Grants 2026 offer an important opportunity for local civil society actors seeking practical ways to bridge divides, foster dialogue, strengthen trust, and promote greater social cohesion across the Western Balkans.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more Global Grant Opportunities here.

The post European Fund for the Balkans Common Ground Program Opportunity and Innovation Grants 2026: Apply for Up to €4,500 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Join the National Service Leadership Council (NSLC): Strengthening AmeriCorps Programs and National Service Across New York City
item.dc:creator: Dora Kumi
item.description:
The National Service Leadership Council (NSLC) is creating a stronger, more connected national service ecosystem in New York City by bringing together AmeriCorps and other national service programs to collaborate, share resources, and strengthen the impact of service initiatives. Through regular meetings, committee work, professional development, and cross-sector collaboration, the Council serves as a hub […]

The post Join the National Service Leadership Council (NSLC): Strengthening AmeriCorps Programs and National Service Across New York City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The National Service Leadership Council (NSLC) is creating a stronger, more connected national service ecosystem in New York City by bringing together AmeriCorps and other national service programs to collaborate, share resources, and strengthen the impact of service initiatives. Through regular meetings, committee work, professional development, and cross-sector collaboration, the Council serves as a hub where organizations can exchange ideas, build partnerships, and collectively advance the culture of national service throughout the city.

Whether you are an AmeriCorps program operating in New York City or an organization looking to expand your impact through collaboration, the National Service Leadership Council offers an opportunity to become part of a network dedicated to improving service programs, supporting members, and creating lasting community impact.

About the National Service Leadership Council

The National Service Leadership Council is a collaborative network designed specifically for AmeriCorps and national service programs that place members in New York City. The Council provides a structured space where member organizations can work together to address common challenges, identify opportunities for collaboration, and strengthen the overall presence of national service across the city. Beyond organizational collaboration, the Council also serves as a resource for New Yorkers seeking meaningful service opportunities by connecting individuals with AmeriCorps programs throughout NYC.

By fostering communication and partnership among service organizations, the NSLC aims to improve program effectiveness while promoting a stronger culture of civic engagement and volunteerism.

Mission of the National Service Leadership Council

The Council is committed to:

  • Strengthening collaboration among AmeriCorps and national service programs.
  • Increasing awareness of national service opportunities across New York City.
  • Supporting organizations through resource sharing and professional development.
  • Building stronger networks between service organizations, alumni, mentors, and community stakeholders.
  • Promoting long-term success and sustainability of national service programs.

Through these efforts, the Council helps organizations maximize their impact while creating more opportunities for individuals to engage in meaningful public service.

Who Can Join the National Service Leadership Council?

Membership is open to:

  • AmeriCorps programs operating in New York City.
  • National service organizations that place members within NYC.
  • Organizations committed to strengthening the national service ecosystem through collaboration and shared learning.

Member organizations participate in regular meetings, committee initiatives, and collaborative events that support both organizational development and member success.

Benefits of Joining the National Service Leadership Council

Organizations that become members of the Council gain access to numerous collaborative opportunities designed to strengthen both their programs and their long-term impact.

1. Strengthen Your Programs

Members benefit from:

  • Increased program visibility.
  • Recruitment support.
  • Access to valuable resources.
  • Sharing of best practices.
  • Collaborative training opportunities.
  • Professional development initiatives.

These resources help organizations improve service delivery, attract qualified participants, and enhance overall program quality.

2. Strengthen Your Voice

By joining the Council, organizations can participate in coordinated efforts to raise awareness about national service across New York City.

Benefits include:

  • Collective outreach campaigns.
  • Cross-sector collaboration.
  • Stakeholder engagement.
  • Stronger advocacy for national service programs.

Working together allows organizations to amplify their impact and communicate the value of service to broader audiences.

National Service Leadership Council Committees

One of the Council’s greatest strengths is its committee structure, which allows member organizations to focus on specific priorities while contributing to broader organizational goals.

1. Collaborative Member Support Committee

This committee focuses on supporting active service members and strengthening participating organizations.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Organizing training and professional development opportunities.
  • Supporting networking events.
  • Coordinating career fairs.
  • Addressing housing support needs.
  • Monitoring retention trends.
  • Improving employment outcomes for members.

The committee helps ensure that service members receive the support necessary to thrive during and after their AmeriCorps experience.

2. Alumni and Mentorship Committee

This committee focuses on maintaining strong relationships with former AmeriCorps members while expanding mentorship opportunities.

Its responsibilities include:

  • Engaging NYC AmeriCorps alumni.
  • Organizing mentorship initiatives.
  • Connecting experienced alumni with current members.
  • Sharing professional resources.
  • Encouraging alumni to champion national service within their communities and workplaces.

By strengthening alumni engagement, the Council helps create a lasting network of service leaders.

3. Leveraging Common Successes Committee

This committee works to increase public awareness of the impact created by AmeriCorps and national service programs throughout New York City.

Its work includes:

  • Developing outreach strategies.
  • Raising public awareness.
  • Promoting the achievements of member organizations.
  • Sharing stories that highlight the impact of national service.

Through effective storytelling and outreach, the committee helps attract future participants, partners, and supporters.

Quarterly Council Meetings

Member organizations participate in quarterly in-person meetings, providing opportunities for:

  • Collaborative planning.
  • Thought partnership.
  • Networking.
  • Resource sharing.
  • Professional discussions.
  • Development of joint initiatives.
  • Planning community events.

These meetings strengthen relationships among organizations while encouraging innovative approaches to national service.

Why Join the National Service Leadership Council?

Joining the NSLC provides organizations with more than networking opportunities. Members become part of a collaborative movement focused on strengthening national service across New York City.  Through shared expertise, coordinated outreach, mentorship initiatives, and professional development, organizations can improve program quality while contributing to a stronger and more connected service community.   Membership also creates opportunities to learn from peers, exchange innovative ideas, address common challenges, and collectively advocate for the importance of national service.

For organizations committed to making a lasting difference through AmeriCorps and other service initiatives, the Council offers an ideal platform for collaboration and growth.

How to Join

Organizations interested in becoming members of the National Service Leadership Council should complete the Council’s membership process and participate in upcoming quarterly meetings. Prospective members are encouraged to engage with the Council to learn more about membership expectations, committee opportunities, and upcoming collaborative initiatives.

To join/apply click HERE

Learn more about this opportunity HERE

Deadline: No application deadline was specified.

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early. Organizations are encouraged to join the Council and participate in the upcoming scheduled meetings.

Conclusion

The National Service Leadership Council is playing an important role in strengthening the national service landscape throughout New York City. By bringing together AmeriCorps and national service organizations, the Council creates valuable opportunities for collaboration, professional development, mentorship, and collective impact.

Organizations that join the Council gain access to a supportive network focused on improving service programs, increasing visibility, and building stronger partnerships across sectors. Whether through committee participation, quarterly meetings, or collaborative outreach efforts, members contribute to a shared mission of expanding meaningful service opportunities and strengthening communities across New York City. For AmeriCorps and national service organizations seeking to enhance their programs while connecting with like-minded partners, the National Service Leadership Council represents an excellent opportunity to collaborate, learn, and lead together.

For more Global CONFERENCES click HERE.

The post Join the National Service Leadership Council (NSLC): Strengthening AmeriCorps Programs and National Service Across New York City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Green Digital Fellowship Programme 2026–2027: Fully Funded European Fellowship for Students and Young Professionals Passionate About Digital Storytelling and Green Politics
item.dc:creator: Feven Erko
item.description:
The Green Digital Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 is now accepting applications from students and young professionals across the European Union who are passionate about climate action, democracy, social justice, and digital storytelling. This fully funded fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity to gain practical experience in digital communications, political campaigning, video production, and online storytelling while working […]

The post Green Digital Fellowship Programme 2026–2027: Fully Funded European Fellowship for Students and Young Professionals Passionate About Digital Storytelling and Green Politics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Green Digital Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 is now accepting applications from students and young professionals across the European Union who are passionate about climate action, democracy, social justice, and digital storytelling. This fully funded fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity to gain practical experience in digital communications, political campaigning, video production, and online storytelling while working alongside experienced campaigners, Members of the European Parliament (MEPs), journalists, and communication professionals.

Running from September 2026 to May 2027, the programme combines online learning, in-person training in Brussels, hands-on campaign experience, and networking opportunities with young communicators from across Europe. Participants will develop valuable communication skills while contributing to meaningful campaigns focused on environmental sustainability, democracy, equality, and social change.

For aspiring digital creators who want to use their content to make a positive impact, this fellowship offers an ideal platform to build professional skills, expand networks, and become part of a growing community of Green digital storytellers.

About the Green Digital Fellowship Programme 2026–2027

The Green Digital Fellowship Programme is designed to empower the next generation of digital communicators by providing practical training in storytelling, campaigning, and political communication.

Recognizing that digital media has become one of the most powerful tools for influencing public opinion and inspiring action, the programme equips participants with the knowledge and experience needed to communicate effectively on issues affecting Europe and the world.

Throughout the fellowship, participants will work closely with communication specialists, campaign strategists, journalists, digital creators, and political leaders who are actively shaping conversations around climate change, democracy, environmental protection, equality, and social justice.

Rather than focusing solely on theoretical instruction, the programme emphasizes practical application through real-world campaign exercises, collaborative learning, and professional mentorship.

Fellowship Duration

The fellowship runs for approximately nine months.

Programme Period:

  • September 2026 – May 2027

During this period, fellows will participate in online workshops, practical assignments, networking activities, and in-person training events.

Selected fellows may also have the opportunity to attend the European Youth Event (EYE) in Strasbourg in June 2027, providing additional exposure to European policymaking and youth engagement initiatives.

What the Fellowship Includes

Participants will receive a comprehensive learning experience combining digital communication training with practical campaign work.

The programme includes:

  • Five interactive online workshops
  • Practical communication assignments
  • Digital storytelling training
  • Political communication sessions
  • Campaign planning workshops
  • Networking with communication professionals
  • Collaboration with fellow participants across Europe
  • Expert feedback on content creation
  • Opportunities to contribute to European communication campaigns
  • Two in-person training sessions held in Brussels
  • Potential participation in the European Youth Event (EYE) in Strasbourg

The programme is specifically designed to help participants build both technical communication skills and strategic campaign experience.

Learning Areas

Throughout the fellowship, participants will receive training in several important communication disciplines.

These include:

Digital Storytelling

Participants will learn how to create compelling stories that inspire audiences while promoting positive social and environmental change.

Topics include:

  • Story development
  • Narrative structure
  • Audience engagement
  • Authentic storytelling
  • Content planning

Video Production

The fellowship places significant emphasis on creating engaging visual content.

Participants will gain practical knowledge in:

  • Video planning
  • Short-form video production
  • Editing techniques
  • Visual storytelling
  • Creative content creation

Digital Campaigning

Fellows will learn how successful advocacy campaigns are designed and implemented.

Training includes:

  • Campaign strategy
  • Digital mobilisation
  • Audience targeting
  • Online engagement
  • Campaign messaging
