Political Communication

Participants will gain insight into how political communication functions within European institutions.

Topics include:

  • Public communication
  • Policy messaging
  • Political advocacy
  • Campaign communication
  • Strategic messaging

Practical Fellowship Activities

The Green Digital Fellowship emphasizes practical learning through real campaign experiences.

Participants can expect:

  • Hands-on communication exercises
  • Collaborative campaign projects
  • Feedback sessions with experts
  • Group discussions
  • Digital content development
  • Networking events
  • Professional mentoring

The programme encourages fellows to develop their own communication style while learning industry best practices.

Networking Opportunities

One of the major strengths of the fellowship is the opportunity to connect with professionals and peers working across Europe.

Participants will have opportunities to engage with:

  • Members of the European Parliament (MEPs)
  • Digital campaign strategists
  • Journalists
  • Communication professionals
  • Experienced content creators
  • Political campaigners
  • Young activists
  • Green movement representatives

These connections can lead to long-term collaborations and future career opportunities.

Fellowship Benefits

Successful participants will receive numerous benefits throughout the programme.

These include:

  • Free participation in the fellowship
  • Professional communication training
  • Digital storytelling education
  • Video production experience
  • Campaign strategy training
  • Networking with European communication professionals
  • Access to experienced mentors
  • Opportunities to contribute to real European campaigns
  • Participation in international networking activities
  • Potential attendance at the European Youth Event (EYE)
  • Certificate of participation upon successful completion
  • Membership in a growing network of Green digital storytellers across Europe

Financial Support

The Green Digital Fellowship Programme is fully funded.

Organizers will cover:

  • Travel expenses for in-person sessions
  • Accommodation
  • Meals during programme activities

In addition:

  • Participation in the fellowship is completely free of charge.

This ensures that financial barriers do not prevent selected participants from taking part.

Who Should Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from motivated individuals interested in using digital communication as a tool for positive change.

Applicants should be:

  • Students
  • Young professionals
  • Based within the European Union
  • Passionate about environmental sustainability
  • Interested in climate action
  • Supportive of democracy
  • Committed to equality
  • Interested in social justice
  • Curious about digital communication
  • Interested in content creation
  • Motivated to learn and collaborate

The organizers particularly encourage applications from individuals who are traditionally underrepresented in politics and media.

Why Apply?

The fellowship offers much more than technical communication training.

Participants will benefit from:

  • Practical experience working on real campaigns
  • Professional mentorship
  • International networking
  • Exposure to European political communication
  • Career development opportunities
  • Enhanced content creation skills
  • Increased visibility within a growing European communications network
  • Opportunities to collaborate with future leaders and activists

For aspiring communicators interested in politics, sustainability, advocacy, or public affairs, the fellowship provides valuable practical experience that can strengthen future career opportunities.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official application form and explain why they wish to participate in the Green Digital Fellowship Programme.

Applicants are encouraged to demonstrate:

  • Their motivation
  • Interest in digital storytelling
  • Passion for environmental or social issues
  • Commitment to learning
  • Communication potential

Early applications are recommended.

Application Deadline

The deadline to submit applications is:

1 September 2026

Click HERE to learn more and fill out the application form.

Conclusion

The Green Digital Fellowship Programme 2026–2027 offers an outstanding opportunity for students and young professionals across the European Union to develop practical digital communication skills while contributing to meaningful campaigns focused on climate action, democracy, equality, and social justice. Through expert-led workshops, hands-on campaign experience, networking with policymakers and communication professionals, and fully funded participation, fellows will gain valuable experience that extends beyond the classroom. Whether participants aspire to careers in communications, advocacy, journalism, politics, or digital media, this fellowship provides the tools, mentorship, and professional network needed to become effective storytellers capable of inspiring positive change across Europe.

Click HERE for more fellowship opportunities.

The post Green Digital Fellowship Programme 2026–2027: Fully Funded European Fellowship for Students and Young Professionals Passionate About Digital Storytelling and Green Politics appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Eric and Wendy Schmidt AI in Science African Faculty Fellows Program 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year AI Research Fellowship at the University of Michigan for African Researchers
item.dc:creator: Feven Erko
item.description:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) continues to transform scientific discovery across the world, creating new opportunities to solve complex challenges in healthcare, agriculture, engineering, climate science, environmental sustainability, and many other fields. To strengthen Africa’s contribution to this rapidly evolving landscape, the University of Michigan has launched the Eric and Wendy Schmidt AI in Science African Faculty […]

The post Eric and Wendy Schmidt AI in Science African Faculty Fellows Program 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year AI Research Fellowship at the University of Michigan for African Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) continues to transform scientific discovery across the world, creating new opportunities to solve complex challenges in healthcare, agriculture, engineering, climate science, environmental sustainability, and many other fields. To strengthen Africa’s contribution to this rapidly evolving landscape, the University of Michigan has launched the Eric and Wendy Schmidt AI in Science African Faculty Fellows Program—a prestigious two-year fellowship designed specifically for African researchers and university faculty.

The fellowship provides African researchers with the opportunity to spend one year at the University of Michigan developing advanced Artificial Intelligence skills for scientific research before returning to Africa for another year to implement AI-driven research projects, strengthen institutional research capacity, and promote AI innovation within their home institutions.

Supported through a nearly $6 million initiative led by the Michigan Institute for Data and AI in Society (MIDAS), the fellowship seeks to build long-term partnerships between African research institutions and the University of Michigan while accelerating AI-enabled scientific breakthroughs that can positively impact Africa and the global research community.

Researchers interested in combining Artificial Intelligence with science and engineering research are encouraged to apply for this outstanding international fellowship opportunity.

About the Eric and Wendy Schmidt AI in Science African Faculty Fellows Program

The Eric and Wendy Schmidt AI in Science African Faculty Fellows Program is a prestigious international fellowship that supports African researchers who are applying Artificial Intelligence methodologies to solve important scientific and engineering problems.

Rather than focusing solely on AI as a computer science discipline, the fellowship encourages researchers from various scientific fields to integrate AI into their existing research, enabling innovative discoveries across multiple disciplines.

The fellowship is administered by the Michigan Institute for Data and AI in Society (MIDAS) at the University of Michigan and is managed with extensive support from the University’s African Studies Center.

Its broader vision is to strengthen Africa’s AI research ecosystem while fostering sustainable scientific collaboration between African institutions and one of the world’s leading research universities.

Fellowship Objectives

The fellowship has several long-term objectives aimed at transforming scientific research across Africa.

These objectives include:

  • Supporting African researchers in integrating AI methodologies into scientific research.
  • Accelerating AI-driven discoveries in science and engineering.
  • Addressing scientific challenges that are particularly relevant to Africa.
  • Building sustainable AI research capacity across African universities and research institutions.
  • Creating lasting collaborations between African scientists and the University of Michigan.
  • Developing future leaders in AI-enabled scientific research.
  • Expanding global partnerships that promote innovation and knowledge exchange.

Fellowship Structure

The fellowship runs over a period of two years and combines international research experience with local implementation.

Year One: University of Michigan Residency (12 Months)

Selected fellows will spend a total of 12 months at the University of Michigan in the United States.

During this period, participants will:

  • Develop advanced AI skills.
  • Learn how Artificial Intelligence can enhance scientific discovery.
  • Conduct collaborative research with University of Michigan researchers.
  • Build international research partnerships.
  • Receive mentorship from experienced faculty members.
  • Participate in summer AI academies.
  • Join interdisciplinary research teams.
  • Work alongside MIDAS researchers and postdoctoral fellows.
  • Develop AI-enabled scientific research projects.

This residency provides fellows with access to one of the world’s leading research environments and exposes them to cutting-edge AI methodologies applicable across scientific disciplines.

Year Two: Research and Capacity Building in Africa (12 Months)

Following the residency in the United States, fellows return to Africa for another 12 months.

During this phase, fellows will:

  • Continue implementing their AI-enabled research projects.
  • Apply newly acquired AI techniques to scientific challenges.
  • Conduct research within their home institutions or research organizations.
  • Deliver AI-related training activities.
  • Strengthen institutional AI research capacity.
  • Promote AI adoption within African scientific communities.
  • Build sustainable research collaborations between African institutions and the University of Michigan.

The second year ensures that the knowledge gained during the residency produces meaningful long-term impact across Africa.

Areas of Research

The fellowship supports researchers working across science and engineering disciplines who seek to integrate Artificial Intelligence into their work.

Potential research areas include:

  • Health and Medical Sciences
  • Agriculture
  • Climate Science
  • Environmental Science
  • Engineering
  • Biological Sciences
  • Physical Sciences
  • Data Science
  • Computational Science
  • Earth Sciences
  • Energy Systems
  • Natural Resource Management
  • Public Health
  • Sustainable Development
  • Materials Science

Researchers from other scientific disciplines are also encouraged if their projects demonstrate meaningful application of AI methodologies.

AI Training and Professional Development

One of the fellowship’s strongest components is its extensive AI training.

Selected fellows will receive opportunities to:

  • Learn advanced AI methodologies.
  • Develop machine learning skills.
  • Participate in specialized AI workshops.
  • Attend summer AI academies.
  • Receive one-on-one mentorship.
  • Collaborate with international AI experts.
  • Participate in interdisciplinary research communities.
  • Improve scientific communication skills.
  • Strengthen research leadership capacity.

The programme is designed not only to improve technical AI expertise but also to prepare researchers to become AI leaders within Africa.

Research Collaboration Opportunities

Throughout the fellowship, participants become part of an active international research network.

They will collaborate with:

  • University of Michigan faculty
  • MIDAS researchers
  • AI specialists
  • Postdoctoral fellows
  • African research institutions
  • International research organizations
  • Academic collaborators
  • Industry and government research partners

These collaborations are intended to continue well beyond the fellowship period.

Institutional Capacity Building

The programme goes beyond supporting individual researchers.

It also aims to strengthen research institutions by helping fellows:

  • Establish AI research initiatives.
  • Train students and junior researchers.
  • Introduce AI methodologies into scientific research.
  • Promote interdisciplinary collaboration.
  • Expand research partnerships.
  • Develop long-term AI research programmes.
  • Increase institutional research competitiveness.

Benefits of the Fellowship

Successful fellows will benefit from numerous academic and professional opportunities.

These include:

  • Twelve months of research residency at the University of Michigan.
  • Twelve months of supported AI research and capacity building in Africa.
  • Advanced Artificial Intelligence training.
  • Mentorship from internationally recognized researchers.
  • Access to world-class research facilities.
  • Participation in summer AI academies.
  • Membership within the MIDAS research community.
  • Collaboration with leading AI scientists.
  • Professional development support through the African Studies Center.
  • Opportunities to establish long-term international collaborations.
  • Enhanced research leadership skills.
  • Increased visibility within the global AI research community.

About MIDAS

The Michigan Institute for Data and AI in Society (MIDAS) leads the fellowship programme.

MIDAS is dedicated to advancing research through data science and Artificial Intelligence while promoting interdisciplinary collaboration across academia, industry, and government.

The institute actively supports innovative research projects that address real-world scientific and societal challenges.

Through this fellowship, MIDAS seeks to expand its partnerships with African universities and research institutions while supporting AI-driven scientific breakthroughs.

Click HERE to read More.

Role of the African Studies Center

The University of Michigan African Studies Center plays an important role in the fellowship.

Its responsibilities include:

  • Supporting fellows throughout the programme.
  • Coordinating professional development activities.
  • Facilitating cultural and academic integration.
  • Assisting with networking opportunities.
  • Strengthening collaboration between African institutions and the University of Michigan.

Who Can Apply?

The fellowship targets experienced African researchers who are interested in applying Artificial Intelligence within scientific research.

Eligible applicants include:

  • University faculty members.
  • Researchers employed by African research organizations.
  • Scientists working in academic institutions.
  • Researchers in equivalent professional roles outside universities.

Applicants should have an active research profile and demonstrate interest in integrating AI into science or engineering.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Artificial Intelligence is becoming one of the most important tools for accelerating scientific discovery.

Africa faces unique scientific challenges in areas such as healthcare, agriculture, climate resilience, biodiversity, infrastructure, and sustainable development.

By equipping African researchers with advanced AI expertise, the fellowship seeks to:

  • Improve research quality.
  • Increase scientific innovation.
  • Build local AI expertise.
  • Strengthen African research institutions.
  • Encourage interdisciplinary collaboration.
  • Produce solutions tailored to African priorities.
  • Expand Africa’s contribution to global scientific research.

Application Process

Applications for the 2027 Eric and Wendy Schmidt AI in Science African Faculty Fellows Program will be submitted through the official InfoReady application portal.

Application portal opening date: 17 August 2026

Once the portal opens, eligible applicants should:

  1. Visit the official fellowship application portal.
  2. Create or log into an InfoReady account.
  3. Complete the Phase 1 application.
  4. Upload all required documents, including:
    • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
    • Individualized Training, Research, and Impact Plan (I-TRIP)
    • Optional Faculty Host Letter of Support (if available)
  5. Submit the application before the Phase 1 deadline of 5 October 2026 (11:59 p.m. EDT).

Applicants who advance to Phase 2 will be invited to submit additional application materials, including revised research plans, letters of support, publications, references, and other required documentation.

Deadline

The Phase 1 application deadline for the 2027 cohort is October 5, 2026.
The Phase 2 application deadline for the 2027 cohort is November 23, 2026.

Conclusion

The Eric and Wendy Schmidt AI in Science African Faculty Fellows Program represents a remarkable opportunity for African researchers to advance their careers while contributing to the future of AI-enabled scientific discovery. By combining a year of intensive training and collaborative research at the University of Michigan with a year of AI implementation and capacity building in Africa, the fellowship creates a unique platform for innovation, knowledge exchange, and sustainable research development. Through world-class mentorship, interdisciplinary collaboration, and institutional partnerships, fellows will be equipped to lead transformative research projects that address Africa’s most pressing scientific challenges while strengthening AI research capacity across the continent. Researchers seeking to leverage Artificial Intelligence for scientific advancement are encouraged to prepare their applications and follow the official programme updates closely.

Click HERE for more Fellowship opportunities.

The post Eric and Wendy Schmidt AI in Science African Faculty Fellows Program 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year AI Research Fellowship at the University of Michigan for African Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026: Funding for Youth Initiatives Promoting Democracy and Human Rights
item.dc:creator: Yvonne Appeynarh
item.description:
The European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026 provide funding for youth initiatives promoting democracy and human rights across Europe. Launched by the European Youth Foundation (EYF) of the Council of Europe, this call for proposals supports ad hoc youth initiatives that empower young people to strengthen democratic culture, promote human rights, develop […]

The post European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026: Funding for Youth Initiatives Promoting Democracy and Human Rights appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026 provide funding for youth initiatives promoting democracy and human rights across Europe. Launched by the European Youth Foundation (EYF) of the Council of Europe, this call for proposals supports ad hoc youth initiatives that empower young people to strengthen democratic culture, promote human rights, develop critical thinking, and actively participate in democratic processes. Through this grant programme, eligible youth organisations can implement innovative projects that address contemporary democratic challenges such as misinformation, disinformation, fake news, barriers to voting, and limited participation in governance, while equipping young people with the knowledge, skills, and attitudes needed to become active democratic citizens.

To strengthen youth participation and democratic engagement across Europe, the European Youth Foundation (EYF) of the Council of Europe has launched the Call for Youth Initiatives “Living and Building Democracy” 2026. This funding opportunity is designed to support innovative ad hoc youth initiatives that empower young people to better understand democracy, develop critical thinking skills, promote human rights, and actively participate in democratic life.

The grant programme encourages youth organisations to design impactful projects that respond to real democratic challenges facing young people while promoting democratic values, civic participation, and human rights education.

Applications are now open for eligible youth organisations registered with the European Youth Foundation.

About the European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Call

The “Living and Building Democracy” call is a special funding initiative established by the European Youth Foundation to encourage youth organisations to create practical projects that strengthen democratic culture among young people.

Rather than supporting general youth activities, this call specifically focuses on initiatives that respond to contemporary democratic challenges and equip young people with the knowledge, attitudes, and skills needed to actively participate in democratic societies.

The initiative recognizes that democracy is strengthened when young people understand their rights, think critically, participate responsibly, and contribute to decision-making processes within their communities.

By supporting youth-led initiatives, the European Youth Foundation seeks to promote active citizenship while reinforcing the values of democracy, human rights, inclusion, and participation across Europe.

Objectives of the Grant Programme

The Living and Building Democracy grant aims to support initiatives that contribute to several important objectives.

1. Increase Young People’s Understanding of Democracy

Projects should create opportunities for young people to:

  • Learn about democratic values.
  • Understand democratic institutions.
  • Explore how democracy functions in modern society.
  • Understand the importance of protecting democratic systems.

2. Promote Human Rights Education

Applicants are encouraged to design activities that help participants understand:

  • Fundamental human rights.
  • The relationship between democracy and human rights.
  • Why human rights are essential to democratic societies.
  • How human rights can be protected and promoted.

3. Develop Critical Thinking

The European Youth Foundation seeks initiatives that help young people:

  • Analyze information critically.
  • Question misinformation.
  • Recognize manipulation.
  • Make informed civic decisions.
  • Build democratic attitudes and values.

This objective is especially important within today’s digital environment where false information spreads rapidly.

4. Strengthen Democratic Culture

Successful projects should encourage young people to:

  • Respect democratic values.
  • Participate in civic life.
  • Engage in dialogue.
  • Promote inclusion.
  • Build trust within communities.
  • Encourage democratic participation both online and offline.

5. Equip Young People with Citizenship Skills

The programme also aims to provide participants with practical citizenship skills that enable them to:

  • Participate in democratic decision-making.
  • Promote human rights.
  • Engage in community initiatives.
  • Contribute to governance processes.
  • Become active and responsible citizens.

Democratic Challenges the Grant Seeks to Address

The European Youth Foundation particularly welcomes projects that respond to specific barriers preventing young people from participating fully in democratic life.

Examples include:

  • Misinformation
  • Disinformation
  • Fake news
  • Information manipulation
  • Political disengagement
  • Low voter participation
  • Barriers to voting
  • Limited youth participation in governance
  • Weak civic engagement
  • Challenges affecting democratic participation in digital spaces

Applicants are expected to clearly explain:

  • Which democratic challenge their project addresses.
  • Why the challenge is important.
  • How young people are affected.
  • How the proposed activities will overcome or reduce that challenge.
  • The expected impact of the initiative.

What Kind of Initiatives Can Be Supported?

The programme supports ad hoc youth initiatives that contribute directly to strengthening democracy and promoting human rights.

Eligible activities may include:

  • Youth workshops
  • Democracy education programmes
  • Civic engagement projects
  • Human rights campaigns
  • Public awareness activities
  • Community dialogue sessions
  • Critical thinking workshops
  • Digital citizenship initiatives
  • Youth participation events
  • Democratic leadership activities
  • Advocacy and awareness campaigns

Projects should demonstrate innovation, relevance, and measurable impact on young people’s democratic participation.

Benefits of the Grant

Successful applicants will benefit from:

  • Financial support for implementing youth initiatives.
  • Opportunity to strengthen democratic participation among young people.
  • Support for innovative civic engagement projects.
  • Promotion of human rights education.
  • Increased youth involvement in democratic processes.
  • Contribution to democratic resilience across Europe.
  • Recognition through support from the European Youth Foundation.

Who Should Apply?

This funding opportunity is intended for:

  • Youth organisations registered with the European Youth Foundation.
  • Youth organisations committed to democracy and human rights.
  • Civil society organisations working with young people.
  • Organisations promoting active citizenship.
  • Youth-led organisations implementing democratic participation projects.

Applicants must ensure that their proposed projects align with the objectives and priorities outlined in the call for proposals.

Application Requirements

Applicants should carefully review the official call documents before submitting their applications.

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Eligibility criteria.
  • Exclusion criteria.
  • Award criteria.
  • Relevance of the proposed initiative.
  • Quality of the project design.
  • Expected impact.
  • Demonstrated response to identified democratic challenges.

The initiative should clearly explain how it intends to address a specific democratic issue affecting young people and provide practical solutions through its planned activities.

Visit Here for more details.

Application Timeline

Applications opened for submission from the end of July 2026.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

1 September 2026 at 23:59 CEST

Late applications will not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested youth organisations should review the official call for proposals, confirm their eligibility with the European Youth Foundation, and complete the required application forms before the application deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to carefully study all eligibility conditions, funding requirements, and evaluation criteria before preparing their proposals.

For more opportunities like this visit here.

Conclusion

The European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026 provide an important opportunity for youth organisations to design meaningful initiatives that strengthen democracy, promote human rights, and empower young people to become active citizens. By supporting projects that tackle misinformation, encourage civic participation, and build democratic culture, the programme contributes to creating more inclusive, resilient, and participatory societies across Europe.

Eligible youth organisations registered with the European Youth Foundation are encouraged to take advantage of this funding opportunity and submit high-quality proposals before the 1 September 2026 (23:59 CEST) deadline. Through innovative youth-led initiatives, organisations can make a lasting contribution to democracy, civic engagement, and human rights education while helping young people develop the skills needed to shape Europe’s democratic future.

Click HERE to apply

The post European Youth Foundation Living and Building Democracy Grants 2026: Funding for Youth Initiatives Promoting Democracy and Human Rights appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
